pi_items.source: Downloaded the real 'student-por.csv' from the UCI repository URL and computed the mean of 'G3' for school 'GP'.
Downloaded the real 'student-por.csv' from the UCI repository URL and computed the mean of 'G3' for school 'MS'.
The task claims a tolerance of 0.15, but verifier.py is entirely missing from the environment, so this parameter is never enforced or used in any scoring logic.
Verified this URL is explicitly downloaded in setup_task.sh and correctly points to the official UCI dataset.

privileged_info_summary: Verified that the task uses the real UCI Student Performance dataset. The expected G3 averages for the GP and MS schools are ~12.6 and ~10.7 respectively, exactly matching the underlying 'student-por.csv' data. I discovered two significant defects in this task: 1) The downloaded dataset URL yields a 'double-zipped' file where 'student-por.csv' is nested inside an inner 'student.zip', requiring the agent to extract twice, a fact not mentioned in the prompt. 2) The file 'verifier.py' is entirely missing from the task environment, meaning the task is completely unscorable because the specified program evaluator 'verifier.py::verify_uci_student_performance' cannot execute.
